meaning: as soon as / depending on / once (it is up to...)

exercises.example_sentence: 準備ができ次第、連絡します。
exercises.translation: I will contact you as soon as preparations are complete.
wrong_choice_explanations: This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜次第' (as soon as / depending on / once (it is up to...)) needed in this context.
This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜次第' (as soon as / depending on / once (it is up to...)) needed in this context.
This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜次第' (as soon as / depending on / once (it is up to...)) needed in this context.

exercises.example_sentence: 結果が判明し次第、報告いたします。
exercises.translation: I will report as soon as the results are known.
wrong_choice_explanations: たら has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where 次第 is required.
てから has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where 次第 is required.
次第で has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where 次第 is required.

exercises.example_sentence: 在庫が入荷し次第、販売開始いたします。
exercises.translation: We will begin selling as soon as the inventory arrives.
wrong_choice_explanations: たら conditional if / when, but 〜次第 has a specific meaning, which is what this sentence requires.
てから sequential 'after' with completion emphasis, but 〜次第 is needed to express the specific meaning required in this sentence.
次第で indicates location of action or means/method, but 〜次第 has a specific meaning, which is what this sentence requires.

exercises.example_sentence: 手続きが完了し次第、送付させていただきます。
exercises.translation: We will send it as soon as the procedure is complete.
wrong_choice_explanations: たら has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.
てから has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.
次第で has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.

exercises.example_sentence: 確認でき次第、返信します。
exercises.translation: I will reply as soon as I can confirm.
wrong_choice_explanations: 〜たら is simple conditional; doesn't convey 'as soon as'.
〜てから indicates sequence; doesn't express immediacy.
〜次第で means 'depending on'; doesn't convey 'as soon as'.
